title: satureja glabella var. angustifolia
intro: Satureja glabella var. angustifolia, also known as mountain savory, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin infections. The health benefits of mountain savory include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and boosting the immune system, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Mountain savory can be consumed as a tea, infused in oil, or added to food and herbal remedies, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
